commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=9d7967ea8a0f5dccb0cd6776f071517ec4d5f076)
commands.review_summary:
The PR replaces the Cost menu’s native subtitle item with a fixed-width hosted SwiftUI row, preserves a native fallback, and adds tests for width and hosted-submenu refresh behavior.

Reproducibility: yes. from source with medium confidence: the native Cost subtitle is the natural-sizing outlier among fixed-width hosted detail rows, but the complete provider-switching symptom was not reproduced in a packaged app during this review.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The concrete changelog cleanup is mechanical, but missing contributor-side packaged-app proof is a human-only merge gate and the owner-authored PR requires normal review.

target.title: Fix Cost row widening merged menu
target.body:
## Summary
- render the Cost detail item as a fixed-width hosted row instead of a native subtitle item
- keep long dynamic totals truncated inside the row while preserving full details in the tooltip
- align the Cost submenu chevron with other hosted submenu rows
- retain the native item path for headless tests and localization refresh coverage

## Root cause
The Cost row was the only provider detail row using `NSMenuItem.subtitle`. Large dynamic totals participated in AppKit's natural menu sizing, widening the tracked menu window. Hosted rows such as Subscription Utilization stayed at the configured width, so their custom chevrons became inset and the widened window persisted during provider switching.

## Validation
- `swift test --filter 'StatusMenu(CostMenuCard|HostedSubmenuRefresh)Tests'`
- `swift test --filter 'StatusMenu(LocalizationRefresh|OpenRefresh|Tests)'` (131 tests)
- `make check`
- `git diff --check`
- structured autoreview: no accepted/actionable findings

## Runtime proof
Packaged-app provider-switching proof will be added after the fresh bundle is exercised with Peekaboo.
target.author: steipete
